archetype_description: Swiss & High-Contrast mixed with Retro-Futurism. A highly technical, precise, and dense 'Control Room' interface for sysadmins, inspired by modern terminal interfaces and elevated Grafana setups.

scale.metric_value: text-4xl md:text-5xl font-mono tracking-tighter font-light text-white
scale.label: text-xs font-mono uppercase tracking-[0.2em] text-[#8A8A8A]

layout.spacing: Tight gaps (gap-4 or gap-6) for high data density.

components.cards: Flat background (#0A0A0A), sharp 1px solid border (#1A1A1A). Absolutely no box-shadows. Corner radius max 4px (rounded-sm).
components.charts: Recharts wrapper. Lines should be 1.5px or 2px thick. Use primary_accent (#00E5FF) for network, pi_brand (#C51A4A) for CPU, and healthy (#00FF66) for RAM. No area fills, just sharp lines.
components.progress_bars: 1px height or 2px height maximum. Very minimal.
components.navigation: Sticky top bar, border-b border-[#1A1A1A] bg-[#050505]/90 backdrop-blur-xl.

animations.micro_interactions: Hover states should lighten the border color to #333333 and subtly change background to #111111.
animations.live_indicator: A small 6px x 6px pulsing dot (bg-[#00FF66]) next to the dashboard title to indicate real-time polling.

accessibility.contrast: Strict APCA/WCAG compliance for data legibility.
accessibility.test_ids: Every interactive element, metric card, and chart container MUST have a unique data-testid.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Initialize the app using .jsx/.js files (NOT .tsx). Ensure Tailwind CSS is configured for dark mode.
2. Build the UI using pure HTML/Tailwind for structural grids, and customized Shadcn UI for specific components.
3. Implement the 'Control Room Grid': Top row for Raspberry Pi host metrics (CPU, RAM, Load, Temp). Bottom section for Docker Containers.
4. Use `lucide-react` for iconography and `recharts` for the line/bar charts. Install `recharts` if not present.
5. Create a robust mock data generator that updates state every 2-3 seconds to simulate a live Grafana-style dashboard.
6. Apply 'JetBrains Mono' for all numeric values and 'IBM Plex Sans' for headings.
7. Strictly adhere to the color palette: Deep black backgrounds (#050505), sharp 1px borders (#1A1A1A), and high-contrast accents (#00E5FF, #FF3366). No generic gray blobs.
8. Ensure every metric card and important element has a `data-testid` attribute (e.g., `data-testid='host-cpu-metric'`).
